title: Section 6
content: 6. Disqualifications for being chosen as, or for being, a 1[Director of a Corporation].--2[(1)] A person shall be disqualified for being chosen as, or for being, a 1[Director of a Corporation[-- (a) if he is found to be a lunatic or a person of unsound mind; or (b) if he has been adjudged insolvent; or (c) if he has been convicted of an offence involving moral turpitude; or (d) if he has, directly or indirectly, any interest in any subsisting contract made with, or in any work being done for, the Corporation except as a shareholder (other than a director) in an incorporated company, provided that where he is a shareholder he shall disclose to the State Government the nature and extent of shares held by him in such company; or (e) if he has any financial interest in any other road transport undertaking. 3[(2) Nothing in clause (d) of sub-section (1) shall be deemed to disqualify the 4[Managing Director] of a Corporation for being chosen as, or for being, a 5[Director] thereof.] 6[(3) Nothing in clause (e) of sub-section (1) shall be deemed to disqualify,-- (a) any officer or other employee of the Government for being chosen as, or for being, a Director of a Corporation; (b) any officer or other employee of a Corporation for being chosen as, or for being, a Director of another Corporation.]
footnote: 1. Subs. by s. 4, ibid., for "member of a Corporation" (w.e.f. 13-11-1982). 2. Section 6 re-numbered as sub-section (1) thereof by Act 28 of 1959, s. 2 (w.e.f. 1-9-1959). 3. Ins. by Act 28 of 1959, s. 2 (w.e.f. 1-9-1959). 4. Subs. by Act 63 of 1982, s. 4, for "Chief Executive Officer or General Manager" (w.e.f. 13-11-1982). 5. Subs. by s. 4, ibid., for "member" (w.e.f. 13-11-1982). 6. Ins. by s. 4, ibid. (w.e.f. 13-11-1982).
